details.description: An outdoor street‑art site at the former NDSM shipyard in Amsterdam-Noord. The central subject is a sculptural arch made from sections of corrugated shipping containers that have been cut and bent into a gateway/shelter shape. The container surfaces are painted in a dripping palette of white with teal, purple, pink and black accents; the interior shows dark “paint-drip” effects. Surrounding ground is a mix of brick paving and industrial concrete with old rail tracks. In the background are large warehouse façades covered with extensive graffiti and murals, consistent with the NDSM Wharf yard. A white car is parked to the right. Lighting is overcast, emphasizing the colorful artwork against grey skies. The setting is an open, industrial square known for legal graffiti walls and site-specific installations adjacent to the STRAAT Museum in the Lasloods building.

details.description: A large, colorful, semi-circular arch sculpture made from corrugated metal sheets sits in an open, graffiti-covered lot. The arch is painted with bright, multicolor stripes and shapes (pinks, purples, blues, greens) and rests on square blocks also adorned with graffiti. The ground is a mix of weathered brick paving and concrete, with wet patches from recent rain. In the background there is a tall brick wall covered in tags and murals, contributing to an urban, industrial art environment. The overcast sky suggests outdoor, daytime conditions. This scene resembles an installation commonly found in Amsterdam-Noord’s NDSM Wharf, an area known for street art and large-scale art pieces near STRAAT Museum and other galleries.

details.description: A striking architectural installation consisting of a large archway constructed from repurposed shipping containers welded together in a curved, tunnel-like form. The structure is heavily adorned with vibrant graffiti in shades of purple, pink, teal, white, and yellow, featuring various tags and text such as "LASER" and "SIEL HOTEL". It stands on a paved public square featuring a mix of herringbone brickwork and concrete slabs. In the background, a massive industrial brick warehouse building (the NDSM Loods) is visible, also marked with graffiti at its base and large red lettering partially visible on the left. A white Volkswagen car is parked to the right of the arch, and the sky above is overcast and grey, emphasizing the gritty, industrial aesthetic of the location.

details.description: Outdoor urban art scene on a cloudy day at the NDSM wharf area in Amsterdam-Noord. In the foreground stands a freestanding arch-like structure made from corrugated metal panels, vibrantly spray-painted with dripping stripes of teal, purple, pink, black, and white and covered in graffiti tags. The arch spans over an open paved plaza of bricks and concrete. Surrounding surfaces and a large industrial brick warehouse in the background are heavily covered with murals and graffiti. A white car is parked to the right; tall streetlights and an overhead cable are visible. The setting is an open square within the former shipyard, now a creative district known for street art walls and installations.

details.description: A circular pavilion/arch made from repurposed shipping-container sections stands on an industrial plaza. The corrugated steel segments form a ring-like canopy and are entirely covered in colorful graffiti and street-art lettering. Surrounding walls and barriers are also heavily painted. In the background is a large former shipyard warehouse with big red vertical letters on the facade, consistent with the STRAAT street art museum building at the NDSM wharf in Amsterdam. The setting is an open paved square with brick and concrete surfacing under an overcast sky.

details.description: A large circular torus-shaped sculpture made from corrugated metal panels dominates the scene. The ring is tilted and forms a freestanding archway with a hollow center, painted with bold rainbow stripes and layered graffiti. The surrounding area is an urban street with brick paving, graffiti-covered walls, and a brick building in the background. Overcast sky, a small graffiti-covered utility box sits near the curb, and a white car is visible in the distance. The overall vibe is a vibrant, outdoor street-art environment with a prominent, colorful sculpture as the focal point.

details.description: The image captures a large, arch-like architectural installation constructed from repurposed shipping containers, located at the NDSM Wharf in Amsterdam. The image is oriented sideways (rotated 90 degrees clockwise). The structure forms a segmented, curved tunnel, created by cutting and welding corrugated metal container sections at angles. The entire surface of the arch is covered in vibrant, multi-colored graffiti and street art, featuring shades of purple, pink, blue, yellow, and white. The structure stands on a paved surface of reddish-brown bricks laid in a herringbone pattern, typical of Dutch streetscapes. In the background, a large industrial brick building (part of the former shipyard complex) is visible, also adorned with graffiti and large lettering (partially visible as 'EA'). A white car and street lamp are visible in the distance under a grey, overcast sky.

details.description: A large, colorful archway constructed from several shipping containers stacked at various angles to form a gateway. The containers are heavily decorated with vibrant graffiti and street art in a wide array of colors including purple, pink, yellow, teal, and white. The structure stands on a paved ground featuring a mix of traditional Dutch brick paving and concrete. In the background, a large industrial brick building is visible, also adorned with graffiti. The sky is overcast and grey. The image is captured in a landscape orientation but presented vertically (rotated 90 degrees counter-clockwise).

details.description: Outdoor urban street-art scene on an overcast day. The focal point is a large circular arch/portal assembled from curved segments of corrugated steel shipping containers, bolted together to form a walk-through ring. The entire structure is covered in colorful spray-painted graffiti with bands of purple, teal, blue, black, and lime over a white base. Surrounding surfaces—including freestanding panels and distant walls—are densely tagged and muraled, indicating a designated graffiti yard. The ground is brick-paved; an industrial warehouse building stands behind the sculpture, and overhead utility wires cross the scene. A small white car is parked nearby. The setting feels like a repurposed dockyard or shipyard turned cultural space.
